docs.text: Welcome to AntakIA AntakIA is a software allowing you to explore a trained machine learning (ML) model and discover what it has understood from the training data. A key use case is to explain (one speaks of 'explainability' of AI) the model to have it approved and certified to be compliant with the law. As a matter of fact, Europe should issue by the end of 2023 or beginning of 2024 its 'AI act'. This text will require companies, when they use AI for certain use caqes, to have their model certified. AntakIA is an open-source project by AI-vidence, a French company, showcasing our regional approach for AI explainability. Besides local and global explainability, we believe in a more relevant and intermediate scale to observe phenomena. We divide the multidimensional space of input values in regions where we can substitute the original 'black box' model with simmpler and explainable models. Because we split the space in smaller pieces, we named our approach from the antique city Antakia (in Türkiye nowadays) famous for its roman mosaics. Installation To install AntakIA, type the following commands: pip install antakia Getting started Open the following notebook to try AntakIA /examples/california_housing.ipynb . You can either lauch the notebook in VSCode or launch a Jupyter server with pip install jupyterlab Then jupyter lab See our tutorial to go through this notebook.
docs.title: Home

As a matter of fact, Europe should issue by the end of 2023 or beginning of 2024 its 'AI act'. This text will require companies, when they use AI for certain use caqes, to have their model certified. AntakIA is an open-source project by AI-vidence, a French company, showcasing our regional approach for AI explainability. Besides local and global explainability, we believe in a more relevant and intermediate scale to observe phenomena. We divide the multidimensional space of input values in regions where we can substitute the original 'black box' model with simmpler and explainable models. Because we split the space in smaller pieces, we named our approach from the antique city Antakia (in Türkiye nowadays) famous for its roman mosaics.

docs.text: Ai-act The AI Act classifies AI systems based on their risk levels, with corresponding regulations for providers and users. Notably, the legislation emphasizes the importance of safety, transparency, traceability, non-discrimination, and environmental respect in AI systems used within the EU. Key elements of the legislation include: Risk Classification: 1. Unacceptable Risk: Systems posing an unacceptable risk, considered a threat to individuals, are outright prohibited. Examples include cognitive-behavioral manipulation of vulnerable groups and real-time remote biometric identification like facial recognition. 2. High Risk: AI systems with a negative impact on security or fundamental rights fall into this category, further divided into two groups: Systems used in EU-regulated product categories (e.g., toys, aviation, medical devices). Systems in eight specific domains requiring registration in a EU database, including biometric identification, critical infrastructure management, education, employment, law enforcement, migration control, and legal interpretation. Generative AI: Generative AI, such as ChatGPT, must adhere to transparency requirements, indicating AI-generated content, preventing the generation of illegal content, and publishing summaries of copyrighted training data. Limited Risk: AI systems with limited risk must meet minimal transparency requirements to enable users to make informed decisions. Users interacting with AI-generated content (e.g., deepfakes) should be informed, allowing them to decide whether to continue use.
docs.title: Ai-act

docs.text: Theory behind AntakIA Dimensionality Reduction The projection in the Values space represents the 8-dimensional values from the dataset in 2D/3D. The projection in the Explanation space is the 2D/3D representation of the Shapley value from the 8-dimensional dataset. The Shapley value represents the deviation of each data point from the average. Methods UMAP creates a high-dimensional graph representation of the data and optimizes a low-dimensional graph to closely match its structural characteristics. PCA is a method that transforms variables linked to each other (referred to as 'correlated' in statistics) into new variables that are decorrelated from each other. t-SNE identifies similarities in the data and constructs a map where similar data points are positioned close to each other, while different ones are positioned farther apart. PaCMAP is a technique for reducing dimensionality that can be applied to visualize data while preserving both the local and global structures present in the original dataset.
docs.title: Theory behind AntakIA

docs.text: The projection in the Values space represents the 8-dimensional values from the dataset in 2D/3D. The projection in the Explanation space is the 2D/3D representation of the Shapley value from the 8-dimensional dataset. docs.title: Dimensionality Reduction

docs.text: UMAP creates a high-dimensional graph representation of the data and optimizes a low-dimensional graph to closely match its structural characteristics. PCA is a method that transforms variables linked to each other (referred to as 'correlated' in statistics) into new variables that are decorrelated from each other. t-SNE identifies similarities in the data and constructs a map where similar data points are positioned close to each other, while different ones are positioned farther apart. PaCMAP is a technique for reducing dimensionality that can be applied to visualize data while preserving both the local and global structures present in the original dataset.
docs.title: Methods

docs.text: California housing tutorial In this exemple, imagine you're are a real estate agent and you want to understand what drives the real estate prices in California. The dataset you will use for this exemple is the California housing which is a dataset to test the regression model. As you see there are 8 variables: MedInc = Median income HouseAge = House Age AveRooms = Average rooms AveBedrms = Average Bedrooms Population = Population AveOccup = Average Occupation Latitude = Latitude Longitude = Longitude See our tutorial to go through this notebook. On this interface there is: (1) The values space (2) The explanations space Two scrolling list on the top: (3) Projection in the VS (4) Projection in the ES Each of the projection list contain different method to view the point: * PCA * TSNE UMAP PaCMAP Three tab on the bottom: (5) Selection (6) Regions (7) Substitution (8) The button slider on the top left turn the two spaces from 2D -> 3D and 3D -> 2D: 1. Selection To select an region of point you can use two tools: * The lasso selection * The box selection Note: by default the lasso tool is selected ⚠ Double-click on the gray area to deselect. ⚠ Try to select an region where the zone coincide with the other spaces 2. Define Rules Each of the different variables represent the main characteristics within the selected area. ⚠ You can adjust rules using the various sliders for a more precise selection. After refining rules, click " Validate Rules " to confirm your selection criteria. 3. Region Operations Click on the selected region than choose from the following options: Delete Substitute Auto-Clustering Delete remove the selected region. Substitution send you to the Substitution tab Auto-Clustering automatically selected every other region : 4. Substitution Choose a substitution model (sub-model) from the available options. Each sub-model is accompanied by a percentage, indicating its functionality. Higher percentages imply better performance.
docs.title: California housing tutorial

docs.text: Installation Installating fron PyPi (recommended) pip install antakia Install from source Clone this repo : git clone https://github.com/AI-vidence/antakia.git Setup the virutal environnement: poetry shell And install the dependencies : poetry install
